subject: [UUI] Solve 2 components in one Docker
commit_message:
[UUI] Solve 2 components in one Docker

1.add postgres env in uui-server
2.add server init sql in uui-postgres

Signed-off-by: dujunyi <hunterdevil@foxmail.com>
Issue-ID: USECASEUI-405
Change-Id: I6868b529a79938d85c00eddb85c0aa009aad4c84

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/5550/ : FAILURE

The Commit title must follow this pattern "[WHAT] A title"

WHAT can be a component name (AAI for example), DOC for documentation, COMMON for common, ...

The title must start with a capital letter (A in the example) and a space must be set between the end of the topic and the actual title: "] A title" ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/5550/ )

It seems that you don"t have a commit body.

You must add at least one sentence explaining the purpose of the commit ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/5550/ )

> 
> And I saw some error in these two jenkins build error about common build.
> 
> 
> 11:16:19 Error: unknown shorthand flag: 'f' in -f
> 11:16:19 Makefile:49: recipe for target 'package-common' failed
> 11:16:19 make[3]: *** [package-common] Error 1
> 11:16:19 make[3]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm3.8/kubernetes/common'
> 11:16:19 Makefile:36: recipe for target 'common' failed
> 11:16:19 make[2]: *** [common] Error 2
> 11:16:19 make[2]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm3.8/kubernetes/common'
> 11:16:19 Makefile:69: recipe for target 'make-common' failed
> 11:16:19 make[1]: *** [make-common] Error 2
> 11:16:19 make[1]: Leaving directory '/w/workspace/oom-master-verify-helm3.8/kubernetes'
> 11:16:19 Makefile:51: recipe for target 'common' failed
> 11:16:19 make: *** [common] Error 2
> 11:16:19 Build step 'Execute shell' marked build as failure
> 
> 
> 
> I guess something wrong occured about jenkins build script in  oom-master-verify-helm3.6/374/ and oom-master-verify-helm3.8/18 .

I used to meet the Error "unknown shorthand flag: 'f' in -f" in my local environment. It is because of the helm version 3.8 uses the built-in 'helm push' command which without '-f' option. So the Makefile failed with this issue with helm v3.8. But helm v3.6 doesn't have a built-in 'helm push' command and need to install an extra push plugin which has '-f' option.
